title: Classical Information Retrieval Models
description: A visual overview of the three main classical models in Information Retrieval, showing their core components and how they relate to a query and document collection.
relationships: Query and Document Collection are processed by an Index
Index is used by all three Models to match queries to documents
Boolean Model returns a set of documents based on logical operators
Vector Space Model projects queries and documents into a vector space and ranks by cosine similarity
Probabilistic Model ranks documents by the probability of relevance given the query
